text.title: Research Repository Operations Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Research Repository Operations methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Repository ops runs on a weekly cadence (sync + tag + dedup + report); monthly cadence runs retention + access review.
rule.rationale: Less frequent ops produces stale data + audit findings.

rule.statement: Deduplication MUST use a stable hash (source_url + interview_date + segment) - never name-only.
rule.rationale: Name-based dedup collides on common names.

rule.statement: Studies older than 24 months MUST be archived (not deleted) unless legal requires deletion.
rule.rationale: Older studies often contain comparative value; archive preserves discoverability.

rule.statement: Access list MUST be reviewed quarterly; stale admin grants are revoked.
rule.rationale: Stale admin access is a top audit finding.

rule.statement: A health report MUST be published every week (even if empty); silence signals ops collapse.
Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec — decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
